subject: Re: Differences between Windows Warp and WarpTools motion correction
body:
Hi James,

Sorry you're running into issues - I suspect these come from hot pixels in
your data.
Warp used to remove hot pixels but Dimitry removed that feature citing that
it was hard to make it work robustly with counting data.

You should be able to avoid issues moving forwards by running ccderaser
<https://bio3d.colorado.edu/imod/doc/man/ccderaser.html> on your motion
corrected averages. I will add a note to the worker logs that are output :-)

Cheers,

Alister


On Tue, 28 Jan 2025 at 07:25, James Zhen <jzhen97@g.ucla.edu> wrote:

> Hello all,
>
> I am working on a FIB dataset and noticed that WarpTools 2.0.0dev31 motion
> correction produced averages with washed out contrast and reduced dynamic
> range, especially for the high tilts. Aretomo alignment of these stacks has
> shifts greater than 2000 pixels for the washed out high tilts, which causes
> streaking in center of the tomograms.
>
> When processed in Warp 1.0.9 with the same equivalent settings, the
> dynamic range of the motion corrected averages is better. Aretomo alignment
> also proceeds normally with shifts in the expected range for all tilts, and
> the reconstructions do not have the streaking.
>
> [image: warp-warptools_motion.jpeg]
> Is there a difference in how motion correction is handled between Warp
> 1.0.9 and WarpTools 2.0.0dev31?
>
> --
